stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {s : String}, (Γ ⇒ # s) → Γ = [# s] ∨ ∃ c : Cand, c ∈ candidates Γ ∧ match c with | Cand.rdiv L B A Δ Λ => (Δ ⇒ A) ∧ (L ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ # s) | Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R => (Δ ⇒ A) ∧ (Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ R ⇒ # s)
∀ {A : Tp} {s : String}, ([A] ⇒ # s) ↔ A = # s
∀ {s : String} {A : Tp}, ([# s] ⇒ A) ∧ ¬ is_atom A → (∃ B C : Tp, A = (B ⧹ C) ∧ ([B, # s] ⇒ C)) ∨ (∃ B C : Tp, A = (C ⧸ B) ∧ ([# s, B] ⇒ C))

∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) → ((Γ ⇒ A) ↔ (∃ s : String, A = # s ∧ Γ = [# s]) ∨ (Γ ≠ [] ∧ ∃ B C : Tp, A = (B ⧹ C) ∧ ([B] ++ Γ ⇒ C)) ∨ (Γ ≠ [] ∧ ∃ B C : Tp, A = (C ⧸ B) ∧ (Γ ++ [B] ⇒ C)))

Introduce an inductive predicate `AtomicCandidateTree : List Tp → String → Prop` with a base case for `[# s]` and recursive cases indexed by `c ∈ candidates Γ`, requiring exactly the two recursive premises attached to that candidate, and prove: `AtomicCandidateTree Γ s ↔ (Γ ⇒ # s)`.

Define a recursive support predicate `support_ok : List Tp -> Tp -> Prop` by `support_ok Γ (# s) := ∃ B ∈ Γ, occurs_atom s B`, `support_ok Γ (B ⧹ C) := Γ ≠ [] ∧ support_ok ([B] ++ Γ) C`, and `support_ok Γ (C ⧸ B) := Γ ≠ [] ∧ support_ok (Γ ++ [B]) C`. Prove that every derivable sequent satisfies it: `∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (Γ ⇒ A) -> support_ok Γ A`.
Define the least context-succedent closure generated by singleton self-support and the three sequent rules, and prove that it coincides with support_ok.

Define an inductive predicate `CandidateTree : List Tp → Tp → Prop` by combining the new atomic-case constructors with one constructor for each right-introduction rule, and prove `CandidateTree Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)` for all `Γ` and `A`.

Prove full one-sided conservativity: if every formula in `Γ` and `A` lies in the left-only fragment, then `Γ ⇒ A` in the full product-free calculus iff the translated sequent is derivable in the left-only calculus; dually for the right-only fragment.

Define a recursive handedness-sensitive closure for arbitrary succedents by combining the atomic exposure predicate with the two succedent-division clauses, and prove that for every Γ and A this refined closure is equivalent to Γ ⇒ A.

Define the deterministic residual atomic state obtained from a sequent `(Γ, A)` by recursively peeling the outer succedent divisions of `A`, pushing each left- or right-division argument into the context and recording the required nonemptiness side conditions. Prove that `HandedSupportClosure Γ A` holds exactly when this peeling process ends in a residual state `(Δ, #s)` with `AtomicCandidateTree Δ s`, and that `SupportClosure Γ A` holds exactly when the same residual state has `∃ B ∈ Δ, occurs_atom s B`.

Define left-only and right-only analogues of `HandedSupportClosure` inside the one-sided fragment calculi, and prove that for any left-only sequent, respectively right-only sequent, the obvious translation preserves and reflects the handed closure.

Define the canonical reconstruction that re-inserts the uniquely determined outer `⧹` and `⧸` succedent peels of `A` above an atomic residual witness, and prove that for every `Γ` and `A` it gives a bijection between `CandidateTree Γ A` and pairs `(Δ, s)` with `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)` and `AtomicCandidateTree Δ s`.

Prove a quantitative residual-search bound: if `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)` and `AtomicCandidateTree Δ s`, then every branch of the atomic search has length at most `list_degree Δ`, so after residualization the nondeterministic part of proof search is depth-bounded by the residual context alone.

Define the atomic residual transition graph on states `(Δ, s)` generated by one-step decompositions of `AtomicCandidateTree`, and prove finite-state recognition: `Γ ⇒ A` holds exactly when the initial state returned by `residualAtomicState Γ A` is accepted by that graph.

Prove a residual subformula theorem: if `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)`, then every formula in `Δ` is a subformula of some formula in `Γ` or of `A`, and the atom `s` already occurs in `Γ` or `A`.

For any Γ A Δ s, if residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s), then every state reachable in the residual graph from (Δ, s) has the form (Δ', s) with every formula in Δ' a subformula of some formula in Γ ++ [A].

For any finite source list Σ and states p q, if every formula in p.1 is a subformula of some formula in Σ and AtomicResidualGraphStep p q, then q.2 = p.2 and every formula in q.1 is a subformula of some formula in Σ.
∀ (Γ Δ : List Tp) (A : Tp) (s : String), residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) → Set.Finite { q : AtomicResidualState | Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep (Δ, s) q }

∀ (src : List Tp) (p q : AtomicResidualState), (∀ B ∈ p.1, ∃ C ∈ src, Subformula B C) → AtomicResidualGraphStep p q → q.2 = p.2 ∧ list_degree q.1 < list_degree p.1 ∧ (∀ B ∈ q.1, ∃ C ∈ src, Subformula B C)

There exist Γ, A, Δ, s, and a state p such that residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s), p.2 = s, list_degree p.1 ≤ list_degree Δ, every formula in p.1 is a subformula of some formula in Γ ++ [A], but p is not reachable from (Δ, s) by residual-graph steps.
∀ (src : List Tp) (s : String) (N : Nat), Set.Finite { q : AtomicResidualState | q.2 = s ∧ list_degree q.1 ≤ N ∧ (∀ B ∈ q.1, ∃ C ∈ src, Subformula B C) }

For every finite `src` and atom `s`, a subset of `{ p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = s ∧ (∀ B ∈ p.1, ∃ C ∈ src, Subformula B C) }` is finite if and only if the values `list_degree p.1` are uniformly bounded on that subset.
AtomicResidualGraphStep is well-founded on AtomicResidualState.
For every p : AtomicResidualState, ¬ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p ↔ p ≠ ([# p.2], p.2) ∧ ∀ q, AtomicResidualGraphStep p q → ¬ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q.

For every `src`, `s`, and `N`, residual acceptance on the bounded slice `{ p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = s ∧ list_degree p.1 ≤ N ∧ (∀ B ∈ p.1, ∃ C ∈ src, Subformula B C) }` admits an internal finite characterization by backward induction on `list_degree`.
∀ p : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p ↔ (p = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ∨ ∃ q : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ∧ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)
∀ p : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p ↔ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p ([# (p.2)], p.2)

For every root state p, the set of states reachable from p is finite and AtomicResidualGraphAccepts restricted to that reachable set is the least subset that contains ([# (p.2)], p.2) and is closed under taking predecessors along AtomicResidualGraphStep.

For every `Γ`, `A`, and `p`, if `residualAtomicState Γ A = some p`, then there exists a finite set `R` exactly equal to the states reachable from `p` by residual steps together with a Boolean labeling on `R`, computed by reverse recursion on `list_degree`, whose value at each state is equivalent to `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on that state.

For every root state p, there exists a unique Boolean labeling of the finite set `{ q : AtomicResidualState | Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q }` such that `([# (p.2)], p.2)` is labeled `true` and any other reachable state q is labeled `true` exactly when some one-step successor of q is labeled `true`; moreover this labeling agrees with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on the reachable set.

For every `Γ`, `A`, and `p` with `residualAtomicState Γ A = some p`, any reverse topological sweep of the finite reachable region from `p` that marks `([# (p.2)], p.2)` as `true` and propagates truth backward along `AtomicResidualGraphStep` computes the same Boolean labeling, and that labeling agrees exactly with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on the reachable states.

For every p : AtomicResidualState, if R is the finite set of states reachable from p, then listing the elements of R in nondecreasing order of list_degree q.1 yields a reverse topological ordering: whenever q,r ∈ R and AtomicResidualGraphStep q r, the position of r is strictly earlier than the position of q.

For every p : AtomicResidualState, any reverse dynamic-programming evaluation on a topological ordering of the states reachable from p, using the local rule 'base state or some one-step successor is labeled true', produces the same Boolean labeling on the reachable region, and that labeling agrees with AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.

For every p q : AtomicResidualState, if Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q and ¬ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q, then there exists r : AtomicResidualState such that Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q r, ¬ ∃ s, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s, and ¬ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r.

For every `Γ : List Tp` and `s : String`, `¬ ∃ q, AtomicResidualGraphStep (Γ, s) q` iff every right-division candidate `Cand.rdiv L B A Δ Λ ∈ candidates Γ` has underivable argument context `¬ (Δ ⇒ A)` and every left-division candidate `Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R ∈ candidates Γ` has underivable argument context `¬ (Δ ⇒ A)`.

For every `q : AtomicResidualState`, `¬ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q` iff every terminal state `r` reachable from `q` satisfies `¬ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r`, where terminal means `¬ ∃ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s`.

There exist `q r : AtomicResidualState` such that `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q`, `Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q r`, `¬ ∃ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s`, and `¬ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r`.

∀ p : AtomicResidualState, ∀ R : Finset AtomicResidualState, (∀ q : AtomicResidualState, q ∈ R ↔ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) → let L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ≤ list_degree b.1); ∃! good : AtomicResidualState → Bool, (∀ q : AtomicResidualState, q ∉ R → good q = false) ∧ (∀ q : AtomicResidualState, q ∈ R → (good q = true ↔ q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ∨ ∃ r : AtomicResidualState, r ∈ R ∧ AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ∧ List.idxOf r L < List.idxOf q L ∧ good r = true)) ∧ ∀ q : AtomicResidualState, q ∈ R → (good q = true ↔ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)
∀ r : AtomicResidualState, (¬ ∃ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s) → (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r ↔ r = ([# (r.2)], r.2))

For every `p : AtomicResidualState` and reachable-region finset `R` as above, if `L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ≤ list_degree b.1)`, then the canonical label on `R` is computed by a single reverse sweep over `L`: start from the all-`false` function and, when processing `q`, set `q` to `true` exactly when `q = ([#(p.2)], p.2)` or some already-processed one-step successor of `q` is `true`; the resulting Boolean function agrees with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on `R`.

∀ p : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p ↔ ∃ r : AtomicResidualState, Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p r ∧ (¬ ∃ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s) ∧ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r

For every root state p and every reverse topological ordering L of the exact reachable states from p, the reverse sweep that labels a terminal state q as true exactly when q = ([# (q.2)], q.2), and otherwise labels q as true exactly when some AtomicResidualGraphStep-successor of q already has label true, agrees with AtomicResidualGraphAccepts on every state reachable from p.

For every root state `p` and every finite set `R` with `q ∈ R ↔ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q`, the subset `{q ∈ R | AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q}` is the least subset `S ⊆ R` such that `([# (p.2)], p.2) ∈ S` whenever that state lies in `R`, and whenever `q ∈ R` has some `AtomicResidualGraphStep`-successor in `S`, one also has `q ∈ S`.
